title: The One After The Super Bowl (2)

jokes.text: ...Cybill Shepherd.
jokes.setup: How big of a star is Marcel the monkey in human terms?
jokes.punchline: Cybill Shepherd - a has-been celebrity comparison
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good economy. The Cybill Shepherd reference works because she was a recognizable name but not A-list, making it a perfect comparison for a monkey actor.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown character
dialogue.line: How big of a star is Marcel the monkey in human terms? Cybill Shepherd - a has-been celebrity comparison

jokes.text: - The one in the director's chair? - Gotcha.
jokes.setup: Which one's the director?
jokes.punchline: The obvious answer delivered straight
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor about stating the obvious. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: Which one's the director?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: The obvious answer delivered straight

jokes.text: It looks like her eyebrow fell down.
jokes.setup: Actress refuses to acknowledge her mustache
jokes.punchline: Creative visual description of facial hair
jokes.explanation: Clever visual metaphor that's both specific and universal. The eyebrow comparison is original and economical.

dialogue.speaker: Makeup artist
dialogue.line: You have a mustache.

dialogue.speaker: Actress
dialogue.line: I do not have a mustache.

dialogue.speaker: Makeup artist
dialogue.line: It's right there above your lip. It looks like a tiny caterpillar doing the backstroke across your face.

jokes.text: Van Damme will be making out with Gabe Kaplan.
jokes.setup: Actress with mustache needs to do romantic scene
jokes.punchline: Reference to hairy 70s comedian Gabe Kaplan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from the mustache setup. Gabe Kaplan reference is dated but apt for the hairy comparison.

dialogue.speaker: Makeup artist
dialogue.line: You can't do a romantic scene with a mustache.

dialogue.speaker: Actress
dialogue.line: Why not? Gabe Kaplan did it for years.

jokes.text: Nice camouflage. For a minute there, I almost didn't see you.
jokes.setup: Someone is trying to hide or blend in
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about their visibility
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic observation. Functional but predictable.

dialogue.line: Sarcastic comment about their visibility

jokes.text: Do you know me or are you just really good at this game?
jokes.setup: Stranger knows Chandler's name
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about guessing names
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler-style observational humor with good delivery setup. Character-appropriate wit.

dialogue.speaker: Stranger
dialogue.line: Chandler?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: Stranger
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I just... I could tell by looking at you.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, you got it. That's my name.

dialogue.speaker: Stranger
dialogue.line: I know. I just said it.

jokes.text: Great job growing up.
jokes.setup: Recognizing childhood classmate who looks different
jokes.punchline: Awkward way to compliment someone's appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler awkwardness. The phrasing treats growing up like an achievement, which is both sweet and weird.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that... Monica Geller?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes! I can't believe you remember me!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Of course I remember you. You look... completely different. In a good way. Like, shockingly good. I mean, not that you looked bad before, but wow, you really... glow now.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Thank you?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm saying this all wrong, aren't I?

jokes.text: I graduated fourth grade and realized I wasn't a pimp.
jokes.setup: Reference to wearing a cap with mirrors
jokes.punchline: Childhood fashion choice interpreted as pimping
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic connecting childhood accessories to adult professions. Perfect economy and unexpectedness.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I wore a cap with mirrors on it. I thought I was pimping.

jokes.text: Back then, I used humor as a defense mechanism. Thank God I don't do that anymore.
jokes.setup: Explaining past embarrassing behavior
jokes.punchline: Immediately uses humor as defense while claiming he doesn't
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic self-awareness. Chandler does exactly what he claims not to do, and the audience gets the joke immediately.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, I know, it was embarrassing. But you have to understand, I use humor as a defense mechanism.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more serious right now?

jokes.text: The Muscles from Brussels? Wham-Bam-Van-Damme?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Van Damme being attractive
jokes.punchline: Rhyming nicknames for the action star
jokes.explanation: Predictable celebrity nickname humor. The rhyming is forced and relies on existing pop culture knowledge.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Van Damme is so attractive.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, Van Damme... Van Handsome.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Van Damme... Van Slam!

jokes.text: Rachel, he totally changed time!
jokes.setup: Asked if Van Damme was good in Time Cop
jokes.punchline: Taking the movie's premise literally as acting ability
jokes.explanation: Clever literalism about movie plots as real achievements. Good absurdist logic.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, he was okay, but I mean, if he could actually time cop, he'd go back and stop himself from making that movie.

jokes.text: He could hear me.
jokes.setup: What's the worst that could happen if you talk to him?
jokes.punchline: Fear of basic human interaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and relatability. The fear of being noticed is universally awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Fear of basic human interaction

jokes.text: Tell him I cook!
jokes.setup: Being pushed to approach attractive celebrity
jokes.punchline: Random accomplishment shouted as appeal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's awkward desperation. The randomness of 'cooking' as a selling point works.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I'll go talk to him.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Hi! I'm Rachel. I can alphabetize!

jokes.text: You don't think I'm cute? I don't know. Do you think you're cute?
jokes.setup: Monica telling Van Damme that Rachel thinks he's cute
jokes.punchline: Van Damme turns it around to ask about Monica's opinion
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection as the conversation veers from its intended purpose. Creates awkward comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel thinks you're cute.

dialogue.speaker: Van Damme
dialogue.line: What about you? Do you think I'm cute?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I think you're very talented.

jokes.text: You can tell her I think her friend is cute.
jokes.setup: Van Damme asked what to tell Rachel
jokes.punchline: He's interested in Monica instead of Rachel
jokes.explanation: Classic romantic comedy misdirection. Predictable but serviceable for plot advancement.

dialogue.speaker: Van Damme
dialogue.line: Tell her I'm interested in Monica instead.

jokes.text: How many times must I touch you on the arm before you ask me on a date?
jokes.setup: Flirtation that's been going unnoticed
jokes.punchline: Blunt question about dating signals
jokes.explanation: Great directness that subverts typical flirting. The arm-touching observation is specific and relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: So when a girl laughs at all your jokes, touches your arm, and asks what you're doing this weekend... what does that mean to you?

jokes.text: And if things go well, maybe this time... I'll get to see your underwear.
jokes.setup: Making plans for a date
jokes.punchline: Reference back to childhood panties incident
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that recontextualizes the childhood incident as adult flirtation. Good structure and payoff.

dialogue.line: Reference back to childhood panties incident

jokes.text: No one was around to hear that?
jokes.setup: Susie's suggestive comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mortified reaction about being overheard
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Chandler's social anxiety. The mortification is relatable and well-timed.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh God, oh God, oh God. She heard me? She heard what I said about her?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, relax. It was just a joke.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A joke?! I basically told her she had the body of a... I can't even say it out loud!

jokes.text: Naturally, you know, I said no.
jokes.setup: Monica claiming she turned down Van Damme for Rachel's sake
jokes.punchline: Obviously setting up that she didn't actually say no
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for the reversal. Monica's obvious insincerity creates anticipation for the punchline.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I turned down Van Damme for Rachel's sake.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You did?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, no. But I'm saying I did.

jokes.text: Jean-Claude, she said yes! I'll see you tonight!
jokes.setup: Monica claiming she said no to protect Rachel
jokes.punchline: Immediately accepting the date when she thinks Rachel can't hear
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of the setup. Monica's immediate betrayal is both shocking and character-appropriate for her competitiveness.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I said no to protect you, Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes, I'd love to go out with you.

jokes.text: She's got the greatest set of... No guys around, huh?
jokes.setup: Joey about to make crude comment about Drew Barrymore
jokes.punchline: Cuts himself off when he realizes women are present
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Joey's crudeness vs. social awareness. The interruption creates the comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Drew Barrymore is so hot, I would—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, hey ladies.

jokes.text: If I ask you to, you'll end up drinking it yourself.
jokes.setup: Phoebe offering to get Monica's espresso
jokes.punchline: Monica's lack of trust in Phoebe's follow-through
jokes.explanation: Character-based humor about Phoebe's flakiness. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'll get you an espresso!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's okay, Phoebe. I'll just get it myself.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm going to do it!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really, it's fine. I appreciate it, but I can—

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Monica, I'm getting you an espresso.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay... thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You're welcome!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? On second thought, I'll just make it myself.

jokes.text: Like you'd drink her coffee after what you did to her with Van Damme?
jokes.setup: Monica being hypocritical about trust
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Monica's recent betrayal of Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good callback that connects Monica's current behavior to her recent betrayal. Well-integrated character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're talking about trust? You're the one who read Rachel's diary!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That was different!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How is that different? You betrayed her trust.

jokes.text: Unless you hook up with a bunch of pigeons.
jokes.setup: Ross canceling for Marcel the monkey
jokes.punchline: Joey's absurd parallel about pigeons
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation. The pigeon comparison is random enough to be funny while staying connected to the animal theme.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, that's not fair! If I had a pigeon, I wouldn't cancel on it!

jokes.text: Stick a fork in me... I am done!
jokes.setup: Ross expressing exhaustion or completion
jokes.punchline: Using cooking terminology for human state
jokes.explanation: Common idiom used literally. Setup for the following vegetarian exchange but not particularly clever on its own.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm done. I'm completely done.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What do you mean you're done? You're not done, you're medium-well at best.

jokes.text: How do you know vegetables are done? Well, you don't. You eat them and you can tell.
jokes.setup: Phoebe doesn't eat meat so doesn't get the fork metaphor
jokes.punchline: Her literal approach to vegetable readiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - taking things literally while missing the point. Good character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, what do you mean 'fork'? I don't eat meat, so I don't know what you're talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! You mean the vegetables are ready when they can be pierced with a fork? That's so specific. Why a fork? Why not a spoon? Or a knife? Or just... your finger?

jokes.text: Okay, then eat me, I'm done!
jokes.setup: Phoebe's literal interpretation of vegetable doneness
jokes.punchline: Ross applying her logic to himself
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the vegetarian exchange. Ross playing along with Phoebe's absurd logic creates the comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know, carrots have to be heated to at least 165 degrees to be considered fully cooked.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that's not really how it works...

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well then how do you know when a carrot is done? When it stops screaming?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, by that logic, I'm not done either because I'm still screaming on the inside.

jokes.text: Did you ever want to do it in an elevator?
jokes.setup: Chandler describing his perfect woman
jokes.punchline: Her sexually adventurous question
jokes.explanation: Setup for Chandler's sexual adventure storyline. The elevator suggestion is mildly transgressive for 90s TV.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My perfect woman would be sexually adventurous and ask me, 'Could we do it on the kitchen table?' And I'd say, 'Sure, but we should probably move the Thanksgiving turkey first.'

jokes.text: How do you know if you wanna do that? You just know.
jokes.setup: Chandler's uncertainty about sexual adventure
jokes.punchline: Joey's confident but unhelpful advice
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Joey's sexual confidence vs. Chandler's anxiety. Functional character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't know, Joey. What if I'm not adventurous enough?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, you just gotta go for it, man. Women love confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: But what if I mess up?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Then you mess up! At least you tried. That's what counts.

jokes.text: Two hundred seconds of passion! We gotta go.
jokes.setup: Chandler worried about time for sexual encounter
jokes.punchline: Joey's precise but brief duration estimate
jokes.explanation: Great specificity with '200 seconds' instead of 'three minutes.' Joey's confidence about brief duration is both boastful and self-deprecating.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How long do you think it will take?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: With me? Three minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Three minutes?!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, but I'm very generous. I usually throw in an extra two minutes.

jokes.text: Well, yes, actually... but they were my Aunt Edna's. And there were three of us in there.
jokes.setup: Susie asking if Chandler's ever worn women's underwear
jokes.punchline: Chandler's detailed explanation of shared underwear experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation. The aunt detail is weird enough, but 'three of us in there' takes it to surreal territory. Peak Chandler oversharing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, there was that time in college when I thought it would be funny to put on Monica's underwear...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: But then I realized it was actually really comfortable, so I wore them for like three days straight.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And nobody even noticed! Which, in hindsight, says a lot about my fashion choices.

jokes.text: You're swell!
jokes.setup: Chandler agreeing to wear her panties
jokes.punchline: Susie's old-fashioned expression of delight
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate slang that fits the character's traditional personality. Mild charm but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: Oh boy!

jokes.text: I made Marcel's favorite dish, banana cake... with mealworm.
jokes.setup: Ross preparing special dinner for Marcel
jokes.punchline: Gross ingredient added for monkey's benefit
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor that shows Ross's dedication to Marcel. The juxtaposition of cake and mealworms works well.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm making a special dinner for Marcel tonight. I got the finest ingredients...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, Marcel hates regular food. I need to add some bugs.

jokes.text: A monkey's gotta work.
jokes.setup: Marcel canceling on Ross for work
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact acceptance using work cliché
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of workplace cliché applied to monkey. The resignation in Ross's voice sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, well, you know, these things happen. Work comes first.

jokes.text: Do you think I can't see you in the TV set?
jokes.setup: Someone trying to hide behind TV during conversation
jokes.punchline: Being called out for visible reflection
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag about TV reflections. The specificity of being caught via reflection is creative.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown character
dialogue.line: I'm not here.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown character
dialogue.line: I can see your reflection in the TV.

jokes.text: Did you just flick me?
jokes.setup: Argument getting physical
jokes.punchline: Incredulous reaction to being flicked
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation. Flicking is such a juvenile action that the outrage is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Did you just flick me?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You flicked me. You actually flicked me.

jokes.text: Quit flicking! Stop flicking! You flicked me first!
jokes.setup: Fight escalating
jokes.punchline: Devolution into childhood-level argument
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how adult friends can regress to childish behavior. The repetition of 'flicking' is inherently funny.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You always do this!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I do NOT always do this!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes you do!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well YOU'RE mean!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm not mean, YOU'RE mean!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Nuh-uh!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yuh-huh!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You're a big poopyhead!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh real mature, Rach!

jokes.text: Happy thoughts!
jokes.setup: Ross trying to stop the fight
jokes.punchline: Peter Pan reference as conflict resolution
jokes.explanation: Ross's desperate attempt at mediation using children's reference. The inappropriateness of the reference creates mild humor.

jokes.text: If we were in prison, you guys would be, like, my bitches.
jokes.setup: Ross successfully separating the fighting women
jokes.punchline: Prison dominance reference
jokes.explanation: Unexpected toughness from typically gentle Ross. The prison reference is dark for his character, making it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! Break it up! Break it up!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's right. We should stop.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Thank you. You know, in prison, the first one to back down is the one who gets...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: ...I'm just saying, you handled that perfectly.

jokes.text: I'm hanging in. And a little out.
jokes.setup: Asked how he's doing wearing women's panties
jokes.punchline: Double meaning about emotional state and physical comfort
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning with good economy. 'Hanging in' emotionally vs. 'hanging out' physically works well.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I'm feeling a little *uncomfortable* right now, if you know what I mean.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: But emotionally? I've never been better!

jokes.text: I don't do the casting.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about her exciting job responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Phoebe assuming he wants acting work
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection. Chandler's attempt at polite conversation gets derailed by Phoebe's assumption about his motives.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what kind of exciting responsibilities do you have at your new job?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I answer phones, I file things, I make copies...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Sounds great.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah! Oh, and if you ever need a job, I could probably get you in there.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah! They're always looking for people. Although, you'd probably want to start in the mail room or something. But you know, you could work your way up!

jokes.text: Because I went to an all-boys high school, and God is making up for it?
jokes.setup: Susie being extremely forward sexually
jokes.punchline: Chandler's explanation for his good fortune
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about sexual karma. The all-boys school detail adds believable backstory while explaining his amazement.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't know how I got so lucky. I mean, one minute I'm sitting there minding my own business, and the next thing I know she's all over me like white on rice.

jokes.text: They do have the shrimp.
jokes.setup: Susie suggesting public bathroom sex
jokes.punchline: Chandler's nervous deflection to menu
jokes.explanation: Perfect nervous Chandler deflection. The randomness of focusing on shrimp during a sexual proposition is very character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: We could do it right here, right now.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, that's... that's a thought. You know what? I'm actually gonna go look at the menu. Could I interest you in some... soup?

jokes.text: But you know what would be even sexier? If you didn't have your shirt tucked into them.
jokes.setup: Chandler showing off in women's panties
jokes.punchline: Susie's practical fashion advice
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy. The image of shirt tucked into panties is inherently ridiculous, and Susie's matter-of-fact fashion advice makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Look at me, I'm a woman!

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: You know, those really don't do much for you. You should try a push-up bra.

jokes.text: I hope you realize this means we'll miss hearing about the specials.
jokes.setup: Being asked to strip in restaurant bathroom
jokes.punchline: Chandler's concern about missing menu information
jokes.explanation: Brilliant priorities humor. In the middle of a sexual encounter, Chandler worries about restaurant service. Perfect character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more naked? And more importantly, could I BE any more out of the loop on what the soup of the day is?

jokes.text: Somebody's been doing his 'Buns of Steel' video.
jokes.setup: Chandler showing his body
jokes.punchline: Reference to 90s workout video
jokes.explanation: Standard 90s pop culture reference. Functional but relies on cultural knowledge rather than wit.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more out of shape?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You could try actually working out.

jokes.text: So you want me to clench anything or...?
jokes.setup: Susie admiring his body
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awkward attempt to pose
jokes.explanation: Good awkward Chandler moment. His uncertainty about how to be sexy is endearingly awkward and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: Wow, look at you! You're really built!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, well, you know... I work out.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: *awkwardly flexes and poses*

jokes.text: This is for the fourth grade.
jokes.setup: Chandler stripped and vulnerable
jokes.punchline: Susie reveals her revenge motivation
jokes.explanation: Excellent revenge reveal. The long setup pays off perfectly, and the callback to childhood trauma is both shocking and satisfying.

jokes.text: I was Susie Underpants till I was 18!
jokes.setup: Explaining her revenge motivation
jokes.punchline: The lasting impact of childhood humiliation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the revenge motivation. The specificity of being called 'Susie Underpants' until 18 shows the real damage done.

dialogue.line: The lasting impact of childhood humiliation

jokes.text: Call me in 20 years and tell me if you're still upset about this.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking why she's still upset about fourth grade
jokes.punchline: Susie's long-term revenge timeline
jokes.explanation: Clever threat structure that mirrors the original timeline. The implication that this revenge will also last decades is perfectly petty.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why are you still upset about fourth grade?

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: Because you made me cry in front of the whole class! Do you have any idea what that did to me?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Susie, that was like, twenty years ago.

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: Exactly. I've had twenty years to plan my revenge. I started small — I made sure you didn't get that job at the ad agency in 1998. Then I spread rumors about your relationship. And finally, I got you to propose to the wrong woman before the right one came along.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, what?

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: Kidding. But I could have.

jokes.text: I hope you realize you're not getting these underpants back!
jokes.setup: Susie leaving with Chandler's clothes
jokes.punchline: Adding insult to injury by keeping the panties
jokes.explanation: Good final twist in the revenge. Taking the panties adds a practical insult to the emotional injury.

jokes.text: What made you make the exception for me? Rachel told me... you were dying to have a threesome with me and Drew Barrymore.
jokes.setup: Van Damme asking why Monica agreed to blind date
jokes.punchline: Monica's supposed threesome interest
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the misunderstanding. Van Damme's casual mention of celebrity threesome shows his Hollywood lifestyle assumptions.

dialogue.speaker: Van Damme
dialogue.line: So why'd you agree to go out with me?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, my friend Rachel said you were really good looking, and my friend Phoebe said you were really smart, and my friend Chandler said you'd be interested in a threesome.

dialogue.speaker: Van Damme
dialogue.line: I would never say that!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, a girl can dream.

jokes.text: That is my favorite sweater! That is my third-date sweater!
jokes.setup: Rachel threatening Monica's sweater
jokes.punchline: Monica's specific dating outfit system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica specificity. Having a designated third-date sweater shows her organized approach to relationships and makes the threat more personal.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, I'm going to borrow this sweater.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No! That's my dating outfit sweater. I have a specific system - that one's for coffee dates, the blue one is for dinner, and the red one is for when I really like them and want to seem approachable but not too eager.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, you have a system for sweaters?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes! And it works. Now give it back.

jokes.text: Give me back my sweater or it's handbag marinara.
jokes.setup: Food fight escalating
jokes.punchline: Monica's creative threat using pasta sauce
jokes.explanation: Creative threat with good specificity. 'Handbag marinara' is both descriptive and threatening, showing Monica's food-focused mindset even in anger.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, you want to play dirty? Fine! I've got a jar of marinara sauce with your name on it, and I'm not afraid to use it!

jokes.text: Yeah? At least I wasn't too chicken to tell some guy he was cute.
jokes.setup: Rachel challenging Monica's courage
jokes.punchline: Monica throwing Rachel's original shyness back at her
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the episode's beginning. Monica using Rachel's original hesitation against her in the current fight works well.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You're not brave enough to do it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh please, remember when you were too shy to even order coffee?

jokes.text: This is crazy! Who can remember why you're fighting in the first place?
jokes.setup: Friends destroying each other's belongings
jokes.punchline: Chandler pointing out the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Standard voice-of-reason moment. Functional for plot resolution but not particularly funny on its own.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So let me get this straight. Monica destroyed my chair, Joey broke my lamp, and Phoebe ate my sandwich. And somehow I'm the bad guy because I accidentally sat on Rachel's hat?

jokes.text: I'll help you throw out your purse.
jokes.setup: Making up after the fight
jokes.punchline: Monica offering to help dispose of ruined purse
jokes.explanation: Sweet reconciliation moment with gentle humor. Monica's practical help with the destroyed purse shows their friendship resuming.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm sorry about before. I shouldn't have said those things.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, I'm sorry. I overreacted.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Look, about your purse... it's completely ruined. But hey, I know a guy who can dispose of it. No questions asked.

jokes.text: I'm sorry I borrowed your gloves.
jokes.setup: Friends apologizing for recent fight
jokes.punchline: Monica confessing to unrelated minor transgression
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's guilt complex. The random glove confession during a major apology is very Monica-like.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, while we're clearing the air, I have to tell you something. I borrowed your sweater last week and I... I spilled coffee on it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I was going to tell you, but then we got in that fight and I figured now was as good a time as any to just rip the band-aid off.

jokes.text: I'm wearing panties.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's naked in the bathroom stall
jokes.punchline: Chandler's specific correction about his underwear situation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the panties storyline. Chandler's matter-of-fact delivery of this absurd situation works well.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not naked, I'm wearing underwear.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, half underwear.

jokes.text: Talk about your bad luck! The first time you try panties and someone walks off with your clothes.
jokes.setup: Learning about Chandler's predicament
jokes.punchline: Joey's sympathetic take on the timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response - sympathetic but missing the bigger picture. His focus on the 'bad timing' rather than the absurdity is very Joey-like.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey man, that's rough. But you know what? The timing could be worse.

jokes.text: Someone's flossing!
jokes.setup: Serious conversation in public bathroom
jokes.punchline: Joey's observation about dental hygiene
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey non sequitur. His focus on mundane bathroom activities during a crisis is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Hi, tushy!
jokes.setup: Chandler bent over in bathroom stall
jokes.punchline: Monica's greeting to his exposed rear
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with good economy. Monica's cheerful greeting to Chandler's butt is appropriately silly.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Hi!

jokes.text: I'm getting heat from the guy in the hot pink thong.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking for someone's underwear
jokes.punchline: Joey pointing out his own scandalous underwear choice
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal about Joey's own underwear choice. The irony of him judging Chandler while wearing a hot pink thong is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Can I borrow your underwear?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? Why would you need to borrow my underwear?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Mine are in the wash.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, you do not want to borrow my underwear. I'm wearing a thong.

jokes.text: Keep your panties on.
jokes.setup: Someone being impatient
jokes.punchline: Chandler using common idiom that's literally applicable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literalization of common idiom. Given the panties storyline, this phrase becomes both figuratively and literally applicable, creating perfect wordplay.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more impatient?

jokes.text: Chandler, can I borrow your G-string? How long you been waiting to say that? About 20 minutes!
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking to borrow guitar string
jokes.punchline: Chandler's admission about waiting for the double entendre opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about waiting for joke opportunities. Chandler's honesty about timing the double entendre is both funny and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Can I borrow a guitar string?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Sure, I've been waiting for someone to ask me that all day.

jokes.text: Mommy! Can't you see what's going on? This man is dead!
jokes.setup: Joey trying to deliver dramatic line in movie
jokes.punchline: His inability to get the line right, ending with wrong word
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's acting limitations. The progression from 'dying' to 'dead' shows his confusion and incompetence.

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.line: Okay Joey, let's try it again. With more emotion this time.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: She's like the wind... *pauses* ...she's like the wind.

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.line: No, no, no. 'She's like the BREEZE.' Try again.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: She's like the... the... *struggling* ...the CHEESE?

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.line: BREEZE, Joey. BREEZE.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Right, right. She's like the SNEEZE.

tmdb_overview: Rachel and Monica compete for the affections of Jean-Claude Van Damme after meeting him on a movie set. Chandler meets a former schoolmate while an excited Joey gets a role as an extra in the movie. Ross and Marcel go on a whirlwind tour of the city.
insight: Two-part finale deflates post-Super Bowl momentum with 4.15 jokes per minute but hollow execution.
